story: Brushstrokes of excitement filled the air as artists gathered in Paris. The Society of Independent Artists unveiled their first-ever exhibition in 1884. Held at the colorful Polychrome Pavilion, it showcased bold new works. One painting that made waves was Georges Seurat's 'Bathers at Asnières', featuring people relaxing by a river on a sunny December 1.
activity_question: Where was the Society of Independent Artists' first exhibition held?
bonus_fact: Georges Seurat's painting style used tiny dots of color to create images.

title: Tiny RNA, Big Discovery
story: Victor Ambros, born in Hanover, New Hampshire in 1953, would grow up to make a huge impact in a tiny world. On December 1 of that year, the future geneticist entered the scene. His groundbreaking work on microRNA, a type of genetic material smaller than anyone imagined, earned him the 2024 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine. Ambros' discovery opened up new ways to understand how our bodies work at the tiniest level.
activity_question: What did Victor Ambros discover that led to his Nobel Prize?

title: A Nobel Physicist's Quantum Leap
story: Tiny particles of light sparked a big discovery for John Clauser. Born in Pasadena, California on December 1, 1942, Clauser grew up to become a brilliant physicist. His groundbreaking experiments with entangled photons unlocked secrets of the quantum world. This work was so important that in 2022, Clauser received the Nobel Prize in Physics!

title: Hockey Hero's Stick-Making Legacy
story: Roger Christian skated into history as a hockey champion and innovator. Born in Warroad, Minnesota in 1935, he grew up to become a star left wing. Christian helped the U.S. team win Olympic gold in 1960. But his impact on the sport went beyond the ice. He and his family created Christian Brother's Hockey Sticks, shaping the game for generations of players.

title: The Doors' Drummer Enters the World
story: Boom! Crash! The beat of rock history began when John Densmore was born in Los Angeles. On December 1, 1944, the future drummer of The Doors arrived. He'd grow up to create the rhythms behind hit songs like 'Light My Fire' and 'L.A. Woman'. Densmore's drumsticks would help open the doors to a new era of music.

title: Little Ricky's Big Debut
story: Lights, camera, action! Richard Keith Thibodeaux made his grand entrance in Lafayette, Louisiana in 1950.
